title: angelica shikokiana var. mayebarana
intro: Angelica shikokiana var. mayebarana, also known as Japanese angelica, is a perennial plant in the carrot family that is native to Japan. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and supporting the immune system. In traditional herbal preparations, Angelica shikokiana var. mayebarana is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in combination with other herbs to create remedies for various health conditions.
